attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	---
2	name: watch-yt
3	description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
4	---
5	
6	# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you
7	
8	You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.
9	
10	## When to use
11	
12	Trigger phrases:
13	- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
14	- "what happens in this YT link"
15	- "describe the video at [URL]"
16	- "summarise this YouTube video"
17	- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information
18	
19	## How to run
20	
21	```bash
22	# Text log only (fast)
23	python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
24	
25	# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
26	python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
27	```
28	
29	Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
30	`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.
31	
32	Optional flags:
33	- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
34	- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
35	  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs
36	
37	## What you get back
38	
39	**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:
40	
41	```
42	[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
43	[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
44	[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
45	...
46	```
47	
48	**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:
49	
50	```
51	/tmp/my-video/
52	  001_00-00.png
53	  002_00-12.png
54	  003_01-05.png
55	  ...
56	  log.txt
57	```
58	
59	## What to do with it
60	
61	- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
62	- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
63	- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
64	- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
65	  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.
66	
67	## Dependencies
68	
69	- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
70	- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
71	- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.
72	
73	## Limitations
74	
75	- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
76	  truncated.
77	- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
78	- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
79	  manually if precision matters.
80

file.content:
---
name: watch-yt
description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
---

# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you

You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.

## When to use

Trigger phrases:
- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
- "what happens in this YT link"
- "describe the video at [URL]"
- "summarise this YouTube video"
- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information

## How to run

```bash
# Text log only (fast)
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"

# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
```

Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.

Optional flags:
- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs

## What you get back

**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:

```
/tmp/my-video/
  001_00-00.png
  002_00-12.png
  003_01-05.png
  ...
  log.txt
```

## What to do with it

- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.

## Dependencies

- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.

## Limitations

- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
  truncated.
- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
  manually if precision matters.

content.text: Now I'll run the watch-yt script on the video.

input.description: Watch YouTube video about Revit annotations via Gemini

content.content:
[00:00:00] A video intro animation shows a golden skyline against a dark, dotted background.
[00:00:03] The speaker, Balkan Architect, begins his introduction, explaining that the video will cover every single tool on the Annotation tab in Revit.
[00:00:12] The speaker mentions he has created similar videos covering the Architecture and Structure tabs.
[00:00:33] The speaker explains that the video will cover every tool on the Annotate tab, including some that viewers may have never used.
[00:00:57] The speaker promotes his website, balkanarchitect.com, showing the homepage with various Revit courses.
[00:01:17] The speaker scrolls through his website, highlighting course content, customized Revit templates, and high-quality Revit families.
[00:01:28] The speaker asks viewers to like the video and subscribe to his channel, showing the YouTube like and subscribe buttons.
[00:01:37] An image of an alpaca appears on the left side of the screen as the speaker says it makes the alpaca happy.
[00:01:45] The speaker transitions to the Revit interface, showing a 3D model of a two-story house.
[00:01:55] The speaker switches to the Level 1 floor plan view to demonstrate the annotation tools.
[00:02:11] The speaker mentions that the project file used in the tutorial is available for download on his Patreon page. An image collage of his Patreon projects is shown.
[00:02:23] Back in Revit, the speaker navigates to the "Annotate" tab on the ribbon.
[00:02:27] The speaker lists the panels on the Annotate tab: Dimension, Detail, Text, Tag, Color Fill, and Symbol.
[00:02:37] The speaker starts with the Dimension panel, specifically the Aligned and Linear dimension tools.
[00:02:52] The speaker demonstrates the "Aligned Dimension" tool (shortcut: DI) by dimensioning a straight wall and then an angled wall.
[00:03:25] The speaker demonstrates the "Linear Dimension" tool, showing how it only measures the horizontal or vertical distance of the angled wall, unlike the Aligned Dimension.
[00:04:00] The speaker moves on to the "Angular Dimension" tool and demonstrates it by measuring the angle between two angled walls.
[00:04:16] The speaker demonstrates the "Radial Dimension" tool on a circular wall, showing the radius.
[00:04:43] The speaker demonstrates the "Diameter Dimension" tool on another circular wall, showing the diameter.
[00:04:53] The speaker demonstrates the "Arc Length" tool on a curved wall segment.
[00:05:32] The speaker explains the "Spot Elevation" tool (shortcut: EL) and places markers on the floor plan, a stair landing, and a ramp, showing the different elevation values.
[00:06:23] The speaker demonstrates the "Spot Coordinate" tool, placing a marker that shows the North and East coordinates relative to the project base point.
[00:07:00] The speaker demonstrates the "Spot Slope" tool on a ramp, placing a slope symbol.
[00:07:14] The speaker selects the spot slope symbol, goes into "Edit Type," and changes the "Units Format" from a ratio to "Percentage" to display the slope differently.
[00:07:42] The speaker moves on to the Detail panel, starting with the "Detail Line" tool (shortcut: DL).
[00:08:00] The speaker demonstrates drawing with Detail Lines, using various draw tools like line, arc, and changing the Line Style to "Wide Lines," "Lines" (which are green), and "Overhead."
[00:08:52] The speaker explains the "Region" tool, which includes Filled Region and Masking Region.
[00:08:57] The speaker demonstrates creating a "Filled Region," drawing a custom shape, and showing how the boundary lines can be individually changed to different line styles, including "Invisible Lines."
[00:10:35] The speaker demonstrates the "Masking Region" tool by drawing a circle over a stair to hide it, using "Invisible Lines" for the boundary.
[00:11:08] The speaker moves to the "Component" tool, which includes Detail Component and Repeating Detail Component.
[00:11:18] The speaker places a "Detail Component" (a US-Universal Beam Section) and demonstrates using the "Bring to Front" option to place it over a masking region.
[00:11:42] The speaker demonstrates the "Repeating Detail Component" by selecting "Brick" and drawing a line, which automatically creates a repeating brick detail.
[00:12:03] The speaker demonstrates the "Revision Cloud" tool by drawing a cloud shape.
[00:12:21] The speaker demonstrates the "Insulation" tool, drawing a line of insulation and adjusting its "Insulation Width" and "Insulation Bulge to Width Ratio" in the Properties panel.
[00:12:59] The speaker explains "Detail Groups" by selecting multiple detail items, clicking "Create Group," naming it "Group 1," and then placing another instance of that group.
[00:13:38] The speaker moves to the Text panel, starting with the "Text" tool.
[00:13:41] The speaker places a text box, types "Text," and shows how to change the text type in the properties panel to adjust its size (e.g., from 2.5mm to 7mm).
[00:14:07] The speaker demonstrates the "Check Spelling" tool, first on a correctly spelled word and then on a misspelled word, showing the spell check dialog window with suggestions.
[00:14:36] The speaker demonstrates the "Find/Replace" tool, finding the word "Text" and replacing it with "ABC."
[00:15:02] The speaker moves to the Tag panel.
[00:15:07] The speaker demonstrates the "Tag by Category" tool by tagging a wall and a door.
[00:15:36] The speaker demonstrates the "Tag All" tool, selecting "Door Tags" and automatically tagging all untagged doors in the view.
[00:15:59] The speaker explains the "Beam Annotations" tool, showing the dialog box for setting up complex annotations for structural beams.
[00:17:05] The speaker demonstrates the "Multi-Category Tag" tool, loading a tag and applying it to a window and a floor, showing that it reports the "Type Mark" parameter.
[00:18:12] The speaker demonstrates the "Material Tag" tool, tagging individual material layers within a wall assembly, such as "Common brick" and "Gypsum Wall Board."
[00:19:04] The speaker explains the Room, Space, and Area tags, demonstrating the "Room Tag" tool on existing rooms in the plan.
[00:19:28] The speaker demonstrates the "Tread Number" tool, which automatically places numbers on each step of the staircase.
[00:20:04] The speaker demonstrates the "Revision Cloud" tool again.
[00:20:11] The speaker explains that "Revisions" have more functionality and that he has a dedicated video on the topic.
[00:20:20] The speaker moves back to the Detail panel to cover "Detail Group" and "Insulation," which he missed earlier. He then returns to the Tag panel.
[00:21:03] The speaker explains the "Multi-Rebar Annotation" tool is for structural projects.
[00:21:21] The speaker moves to the "Keynote" tool, explaining the Keynoting Settings and demonstrating placing a "Material Keynote" by tagging a wall, which brings up the Keynotes dialog to select a specific note.
[00:22:00] The speaker moves to the "Color Fill" panel.
[00:22:04] The speaker demonstrates the "Color Fill Legend" by placing it in the view, choosing to color "Rooms" by "Name." The rooms are colored, and a legend is created.
[00:22:31] To demonstrate the legend updating, the speaker changes the name of "Room 1" to "Room1," which creates a new entry and color in the legend and updates the room's fill color.
[00:22:47] The speaker switches to a different Revit project showing a plumbing layout to demonstrate the "Pipe Legend" and "Duct Legend."
[00:23:05] The speaker places a "Pipe Legend" and chooses the "Pipe Color Fill - Size" scheme. This colors all pipes in the view according to their size and creates a corresponding legend.
[00:23:40] The speaker moves to the "Symbol" panel.
[00:23:41] The speaker demonstrates the "Symbol" tool, placing a "Centerline" symbol and a "North Arrow" symbol.
[00:24:25] The speaker demonstrates the "Stair Path" symbol, adding an "UP" arrow to the staircase.
[00:24:33] The speaker demonstrates the "Span Direction" symbol on the previously created floor slab.
[00:24:56] The speaker concludes the overview of all tools on the Annotate tab.
[00:25:33] The video ends with an outro screen showing suggested videos and a link to the Balkan Architect website.

input.prompt:
Search the bim-ai codebase at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai for all annotation-related features. I need to understand what annotation tools are implemented.

The Revit Annotate tab has these categories of tools:
- Dimensions: Aligned, Linear, Angular, Radial, Diameter, Arc Length, Spot Elevation, Spot Coordinate, Spot Slope
- Detail: Detail Line, Filled Region, Masking Region, Detail Component, Repeating Detail Component, Revision Cloud, Insulation, Detail Groups
- Text: Text, Check Spelling, Find/Replace
- Tags: Tag by Category, Tag All, Beam Annotations, Multi-Category Tag, Material Tag, Room Tag, Tread Number, Multi-Rebar Annotation, Keynote
- Color Fill: Color Fill Legend, Pipe Legend, Duct Legend
- Symbols: Symbol, Stair Path, Span Direction

Search for:
1. Any files mentioning "annotation", "dimension", "tag", "detail line", "filled region", "masking region", "keynote", "color fill", "spot elevation", "spot coordinate", "stair path", "span direction"
2. Any command files, model classes, or UI components related to these concepts
3. Look in app/bim_ai/, packages/web/src/, packages/cli/

Give me a thorough report of which annotation features exist in the codebase, with file paths. Search breadth: very thorough.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile the final comprehensive report:

## Annotation Features in BIM-AI Codebase - Comprehensive Report

I've completed a thorough search of the bim-ai codebase for annotation-related features. Here's what I found:

### **IMPLEMENTED ANNOTATION FEATURES**

#### **1. Dimensions**
- **Element**: `DimensionElem`
- **Command**: `CreateDimensionCmd`
- **Files**:

### **ANNOTATION TOOLS IN TOOL REGISTRY**

- Symbol
- Stair Path
- Span Direction

### **COMMAND DISPATCH HANDLERS**

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` - Handles view-local annotations (tags, detail lines, detail regions, text notes, sections, elevations, callouts, plan regions)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_core.py` - Handles level-based dimensions and grid lines
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` - Central command routing and element imports

This comprehensive implementation covers the core annotation workflow (dimensions, tags, detail geometry, and text) with auto-generation capabilities, but lacks the specialized dimension types, spot annotations, and advanced annotation features that Revit provides.

524	class DimensionElem(BaseModel):
525	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
526	    kind: Literal["dimension"] = "dimension"
527	    id: str
528	    name: str = "Dimension"
529	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
530	    a_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="aMm")
531	    b_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="bMm")
532	    offset_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="offsetMm")
533	    text_offset_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="textOffsetMm")
534	    ref_element_id_a: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="refElementIdA")
535	    ref_element_id_b: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="refElementIdB")
536	    tag_definition_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="tagDefinitionId")
537	    auto_generated: bool = Field(default=False, alias="autoGenerated")
538	    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
539	
540	
541	class PlacedTagElem(BaseModel):
542	    """PLN-01 / ANN-01 — view-local placed tag (room / door / window)."""
543	
544	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
545	    kind: Literal["placed_tag"] = "placed_tag"
546	    id: str
547	    host_element_id: str = Field(alias="hostElementId")
548	    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
549	    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
550	    tag_definition_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="tagDefinitionId")
551	    text_override: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="textOverride")
552	    auto_generated: bool = Field(default=False, alias="autoGenerated")
553	
554	
555	DetailLineStyle = Literal["solid", "dashed", "dotted"]
556	
557	
558	class DetailLineElem(BaseModel):
559	    """ANN-01 — view-local 2D polyline annotation."""
560	
561	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
562	    kind: Literal["detail_line"] = "detail_line"
563	    id: str
564	    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
565	    points_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="pointsMm")
566	    stroke_mm: float = Field(default=1.0, alias="strokeMm", gt=0)
567	    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
568	    style: DetailLineStyle = Field(default="solid")
569	
570	
571	DetailRegionFillPattern = Literal["solid", "hatch_45", "hatch_90", "crosshatch", "dots"]
572	
573	
574	class DetailRegionElem(BaseModel):
575	    """ANN-01 / ANN-V3-01 — view-local 2D filled region annotation."""
576	
577	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
578	    kind: Literal["detail_region"] = "detail_region"
579	    id: str
580	    # v2 fields (ANN-01) — kept for backward compat
581	    host_view_id: str = Field(default="", alias="hostViewId")
582	    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="boundaryMm")
583	    fill_colour: str = Field(default="#cccccc", alias="fillColour")
584	    fill_pattern: DetailRegionFillPattern = Field(default="solid", alias="fillPattern")
585	    stroke_mm: float = Field(default=0.5, alias="strokeMm", ge=0)

def try_apply_documentation_command(doc, cmd, *, source_provider=None) -> bool:
    els = doc.elements
    match cmd:
        case CreatePlanRegionCmd():
            pid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if pid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{pid}'")
            if cmd.level_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.level_id], LevelElem):
                raise ValueError("createPlanRegion.levelId must reference Level")

case ClearAutoGeneratedAnnotationsCmd():
            scope = cmd.scope
            host_view_id = cmd.host_view_id
            to_delete: list[str] = []
            for eid, el in els.items():
                if scope in {"dimensions", "both"} and isinstance(el, DimensionElem):
                    if el.auto_generated:
                        to_delete.append(eid)
                if scope in {"tags", "both"} and isinstance(el, PlacedTagElem):
                    if el.auto_generated and (
                        host_view_id is None or el.host_view_id == host_view_id
                    ):
                        to_delete.append(eid)
            for eid in to_delete:
                del els[eid]

        case CreateDetailLineCmd():
            did = cmd.id or new_id()
            if did in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{did}'")
            view = els.get(cmd.host_view_id)
            if view is None or view.kind not in {"plan_view", "section_cut", "elevation_view"}:
                raise ValueError(
                    "createDetailLine.hostViewId must reference plan_view/section_cut/elevation_view"
                )
            if len(cmd.points_mm) < 2:
                raise ValueError("createDetailLine.pointsMm must contain at least 2 points")
            els[did] = DetailLineElem(
                kind="detail_line",
                id=did,
                host_view_id=cmd.host_view_id,
                points_mm=list(cmd.points_mm),
                stroke_mm=cmd.stroke_mm,
                colour=cmd.colour,
                style=cmd.style,
            )

        case CreateDetailRegionCmd():
            did = cmd.id or new_id()
            if did in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{did}'")
            view = els.get(cmd.host_view_id)
            if view is None or view.kind not in {"plan_view", "section_cut", "elevation_view"}:
                raise ValueError(
                    "createDetailRegion.hostViewId must reference plan_view/section_cut/elevation_view"
                )
            if len(cmd.boundary_mm) < 3:
                raise ValueError("createDetailRegion.boundaryMm must contain at least 3 points")
            els[did] = DetailRegionElem(
                kind="detail_region",
                id=did,
                host_view_id=cmd.host_view_id,
                boundary_mm=list(cmd.boundary_mm),
                fill_colour=cmd.fill_colour,
                fill_pattern=cmd.fill_pattern,
                stroke_mm=cmd.stroke_mm,
                stroke_colour=cmd.stroke_colour,
            )

        case CreateTextNoteCmd():
            tid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if tid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{tid}'")
            view = els.get(cmd.host_view_id)
            if view is None or view.kind not in {"plan_view", "section_cut", "elevation_view"}:
                raise ValueError(
                    "createTextNote.hostViewId must reference plan_view/section_cut/elevation_view"
                )
            if not cmd.text:
                raise ValueError("createTextNote.text must be non-empty")
            els[tid] = TextNoteElem(
                kind="text_note",
                id=tid,
                host_view_id=cmd.host_view_id,
                position_mm=cmd.position_mm,
                text=cmd.text,
                font_size_mm=cmd.font_size_mm,
                anchor=cmd.anchor,
                rotation_deg=cmd.rotation_deg,
                colour=cmd.colour,
            )

        case CreateReferencePlaneCmd():
            rpid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if rpid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{rpid}'")
            lvl = els.get(cmd.level_id)
            if not isinstance(lvl, LevelElem):
                raise ValueError("createReferencePlane.levelId must reference an existing Level")
            if cmd.start_mm.x_mm == cmd.end_mm.x_mm and cmd.start_mm.y_mm == cmd.end_mm.y_mm:
                raise ValueError("createReferencePlane.startMm and endMm must differ")
            if cmd.is_work_plane:
                # Only one ref plane per level may be the active work plane.
                for other_id, other in list(els.items()):
                    if (
                        isinstance(other, ReferencePlaneElem)
                        and other.level_id == cmd.level_id
                        and other.is_work_plane
                    ):
                        els[other_id] = other.model_copy(update={"is_work_plane": False})
            els[rpid] = ReferencePlaneElem(
                kind="reference_plane",
                id=rpid,
                name=cmd.name,
                level_id=cmd.level_id,
                start_mm=cmd.start_mm,
                end_mm=cmd.end_mm,
                is_work_plane=cmd.is_work_plane,
            )

        case UpdateReferencePlaneCmd():
            rp = els.get(cmd.reference_plane_id)
            if not isinstance(rp, ReferencePlaneElem):
                raise ValueError(
                    "updateReferencePlane.referencePlaneId must reference a reference_plane"
                )
            new_start = cmd.start_mm if cmd.start_mm is not None else rp.start_mm
            new_end = cmd.end_mm if cmd.end_mm is not None else rp.end_mm
            if new_start.x_mm == new_end.x_mm and new_start.y_mm == new_end.y_mm:
                raise ValueError("updateReferencePlane: startMm and endMm must differ")

class MaskingRegionElem(BaseModel):
    """KRN-10 — view-local 2D filled region that occludes underlying linework.

    Renders on plan / section / elevation as an opaque polygon above element
    linework but below text/dimension annotations. Not visible in 3D.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["masking_region"] = "masking_region"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    fill_color: str = Field(default="#ffffff", alias="fillColor")


class SelectionSetRuleSpec(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a single rule in a selection set's filter list.

    ``link_scope`` controls which models the rule resolves against:

    * ``'host'`` (default) — only host elements match.
    * ``'all_links'`` — every ``link_model`` element is walked; matching source
      elements are included with their AABBs transformed by the link's
      ``positionMm`` + ``rotationDeg``.
    * ``{ 'specificLinkId': '<link-id>' }`` — restrict matches to a single
      link.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    field: Literal["category", "level", "typeName"]
    operator: Literal["equals", "contains"]
    value: str
    link_scope: str | dict[str, str] | None = Field(default=None, alias="linkScope")


class SelectionSetElem(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a named filter that resolves to a list of element ids.

    Stored in the model so it can be referenced by clash tests and (later)
    schedules. The element itself is non-graphical.

file.content:
# ruff: noqa: I001

from bim_ai.engine import (
    Command,
    Document,
    SourceDocProvider,
    _enforce_linked_readonly,
    _enforce_pin_block,
)

from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_core import try_apply_core_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_properties import try_apply_properties_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_building import try_apply_building_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_structure import try_apply_structure_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_documentation import try_apply_documentation_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_coordination import try_apply_coordination_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_viewsheets import try_apply_viewsheets_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_siteassets import try_apply_siteassets_command
from bim_ai.engine_dispatch_presentation import try_apply_presentation_command


def apply_inplace(
    doc: Document,
    cmd: Command,
    *,
    source_provider: SourceDocProvider | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Apply ``cmd`` to ``doc`` in place.

    ``source_provider`` is optional: only ``RunClashTestCmd`` (FED-02) consults
    it to walk linked source documents for cross-link clash detection. All
    other commands ignore it. The route layer threads a real DB-backed
    provider; tests and intra-host paths can omit it.
    """
    els = doc.elements
    _enforce_linked_readonly(cmd)
    _enforce_pin_block(els, cmd)
    for handler in (
        try_apply_core_command,
        try_apply_properties_command,
        try_apply_building_command,
        try_apply_structure_command,
        try_apply_documentation_command,
        try_apply_coordination_command,
        try_apply_viewsheets_command,
        try_apply_siteassets_command,
        try_apply_presentation_command,
    ):
        if handler(doc, cmd, source_provider=source_provider):
            break

    # KRN-08: areas track a derived computedAreaSqMm. Recompute after every
    # command apply so create/update/delete of areas (and shafts that affect
    # `net` rule-set deductions) keep the value current.
    from bim_ai.area_calculation import recompute_all_areas

    recompute_all_areas(els)

input.content:
# Revit Annotation Tab — Parity Tracker

Source: [Balkan Architect — Every Tool on the Annotate Tab](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HTtKD5ZkJfk)

## Status legend

- ✅ Implemented
- ⚠️ Partial — core model exists, rendering / UI incomplete
- ❌ Missing — tracked for implementation
- 🚫 N/A — MEP, structural-rebar, or browser-native; not in bim-ai scope

---

## Dimension panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Aligned Dimension | ✅ | `DimensionElem` / `CreateDimensionCmd` | 2-point aligned dimension chain; shortcut DI |
| Linear Dimension | ✅ | same — horizontal/vertical locked via UI | functionally equivalent to our dimension tool |
| Angular Dimension | ❌ | — | measures angle between two lines; tracked in `ann/angular-dimension` |
| Radial Dimension | ❌ | — | radius of circular/arc walls; low priority |
| Diameter Dimension | ❌ | — | diameter variant; low priority |
| Arc Length | ❌ | — | arc-segment length; low priority |
| Spot Elevation | ❌ | — | diamond + elevation text at a picked point; tracked in `ann/spot-elevation` |
| Spot Coordinate | ❌ | — | N/E coordinate annotation; lower priority |
| Spot Slope | ❌ | — | slope % / ratio annotation; lower priority |

---

## Detail panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Detail Line | ✅ | `DetailLineElem` / `CreateDetailLineCmd` | solid / dashed / dotted styles; shortcut DL |
| Filled Region | ✅ | `DetailRegionElem` / `CreateDetailRegionCmd` | solid, hatch_45/90, crosshatch, dots fill patterns |
| Masking Region | ✅ | `MaskingRegionElem` / `CreateMaskingRegionCmd` | opaque white polygon; occludes linework |
| Detail Component | ❌ | — | place a 2D family instance in a view; blocked on family library |
| Repeating Detail Component | ❌ | — | pattern-repeated 2D family; blocked on family library |
| Revision Cloud | ❌ | — | cloud-shaped boundary; tracked in `ann/revision-cloud` |
| Insulation | ❌ | — | zigzag insulation linework annotation; lower priority |
| Detail Groups | ❌ | — | named group of detail items; lower priority |

---

## Text panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Text | ✅ | `TextNoteElem` / `CreateTextNoteCmd` | 9-point anchor, rotation, font size, colour |
| Check Spelling | 🚫 | — | browser-native; not applicable in canvas context |
| Find / Replace | 🚫 | — | not applicable for view-local annotations |

---

## Tag panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tag by Category | ✅ | `PlaceTagCmd` | door / window / room; auto-positioned |
| Tag All | ✅ | `generateAutoTags` in `AnnotateRibbon` | idempotent; clears previous run first |
| Beam Annotations | 🚫 | — | structural beam annotation; out of scope |
| Multi-Category Tag | ❌ | — | tags any element via Type Mark; lower priority |
| Material Tag | ❌ | — | tags individual wall-layer material; lower priority |
| Room Tag | ✅ | `PlaceTagCmd` + `TagDefinitionElem` (kind: room) | area + name label |
| Tread Number | ❌ | — | auto-numbers stair treads; lower priority |
| Multi-Rebar Annotation | 🚫 | — | structural rebar; out of scope |
| Keynote | ❌ | — | links elements to keynote database; lower priority |

---

## Color Fill panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Color Fill Legend | ⚠️ | `RoomColorSchemeElem` / `UpsertRoomColorSchemeCmd` | colors rooms in plan; no separate legend viewport yet |
| Pipe Legend | 🚫 | — | MEP; out of scope |
| Duct Legend | 🚫 | — | MEP; out of scope |

---

## Symbol panel

| Revit tool | Status | bim-ai equivalent | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Symbol | ❌ | — | North Arrow, Centerline, etc.; tracked in `ann/annotation-symbol` |
| Stair Path | ❌ | — | UP/DOWN arrow on stair; tracked in `ann/annotation-symbol` |
| Span Direction | ❌ | — | floor slab span arrow; lower priority |

---

## Implementation branches

| Branch | Feature | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `ann/spot-elevation` | `SpotElevationElem` — diamond + elevation label at a point | done |
| `ann/revision-cloud` | `RevisionCloudElem` — cloud-shaped closed annotation | done |
| `ann/angular-dimension` | `AngularDimensionElem` — arc-based angle measurement | done |
| `ann/annotation-symbol` | `AnnotationSymbolElem` — North Arrow, Stair Path, Centerline | done |

content.text:
All done. Here's what was delivered:

**`spec/revit-annotations.md`** — full parity tracker covering all 34 tools from the Revit Annotate tab, categorized as ✅ implemented, ⚠️ partial, ❌ missing, or 🚫 N/A.

**4 new annotation element types**, each implemented end-to-end (backend element + command + dispatch handler + TypeScript types + frontend primitive extraction + ribbon button):

- `spot_elevation` (ANN-02) — diamond + elevation value at a point; "Spot Elev." button
- `revision_cloud` (ANN-03) — closed cloud-shaped boundary for marking revisions; "Rev. Cloud" button
- `angular_dimension` (ANN-04) — arc-based angle measurement between two rays from a vertex; "∠ Dim" button
- `annotation_symbol` (ANN-05) — North Arrow, Stair Path (up/down), Centerline symbols; "N Arrow" and "Stair ↑" buttons

The following Revit tools were assessed as not applicable to our scope: Pipe/Duct Legend (MEP), Beam/Rebar Annotations (structural), and Check Spelling/Find-Replace (browser-native). Lower-priority items (radial/diameter dimensions, keynote, material tag, tread number, span direction) are documented in the tracker for future sprints.

input.description: Implement radial, diameter, and arc-length dimensions
input.prompt:
Implement three new dimension annotation types for bim-ai. Follow the exact same pattern as `AngularDimensionElem` / `CreateAngularDimensionCmd` added in the previous sprint (it's already in the codebase — study it as a template).

The three features:

---

## 1. Radial Dimension (`radial_dimension`)

Shows the radius from a center point to a point on an arc.

### elements.py — add `RadialDimensionElem` near other dimension types:
```python
class RadialDimensionElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-06 — radial dimension from arc center to arc point."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["radial_dimension"] = "radial_dimension"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    arc_point_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="arcPointMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
    auto_generated: bool = Field(default=False, alias="autoGenerated")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateRadialDimensionCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-06 — radial dimension."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createRadialDimension"] = "createRadialDimension"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    arc_point_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="arcPointMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### Handler validation: center_mm and arc_point_mm must differ (use math.isclose on both coords).

---

## 2. Diameter Dimension (`diameter_dimension`)

Shows the diameter across a circle (2 × radius).

### elements.py:
```python
class DiameterDimensionElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-07 — diameter dimension across a circle."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["diameter_dimension"] = "diameter_dimension"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    arc_point_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="arcPointMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
    auto_generated: bool = Field(default=False, alias="autoGenerated")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateDiameterDimensionCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-07 — diameter dimension."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createDiameterDimension"] = "createDiameterDimension"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    arc_point_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="arcPointMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

---

## 3. Arc Length Dimension (`arc_length_dimension`)

Shows the length of an arc segment defined by center + radius + two angles.

### elements.py:
```python
class ArcLengthDimensionElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-08 — arc length dimension on a curved segment."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["arc_length_dimension"] = "arc_length_dimension"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    radius_mm: float = Field(alias="radiusMm", gt=0)
    start_angle_deg: float = Field(alias="startAngleDeg")
    end_angle_deg: float = Field(alias="endAngleDeg")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
    auto_generated: bool = Field(default=False, alias="autoGenerated")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateArcLengthDimensionCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-08 — arc length dimension."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createArcLengthDimension"] = "createArcLengthDimension"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    center_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="centerMm")
    radius_mm: float = Field(alias="radiusMm")
    start_angle_deg: float = Field(alias="startAngleDeg")
    end_angle_deg: float = Field(alias="endAngleDeg")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### Handler validation: start_angle_deg must not equal end_angle_deg (use math.isclose), radius_mm must be > 0.

---

## What files to modify

Same 7-file pattern as before:

1. `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — add the 3 element classes
2. `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — add the 3 command classes + add all 3 to the `Command` union
3. `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — import all 6 new names (3 elements + 3 commands) alphabetically
4. `app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — import + add 3 `case` handlers
5. `packages/core/src/index.ts` — add 3 new kind strings to the union + 3 element type blocks
6. `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — add 3 primitive types + extraction branches
7. `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — add 3 buttons: "Radial Dim", "Dia. Dim", "Arc Len."

## Frontend primitives

```typescript
export type RadialDimensionPrimitive = {
  kind: 'radial_dimension'; id: string;
  centerMm: XY; arcPointMm: XY; colour: string;
};
export type DiameterDimensionPrimitive = {
  kind: 'diameter_dimension'; id: string;
  centerMm: XY; arcPointMm: XY; colour: string;
};
export type ArcLengthDimensionPrimitive = {
  kind: 'arc_length_dimension'; id: string;
  centerMm: XY; radiusMm: number;
  startAngleDeg: number; endAngleDeg: number; colour: string;
};
```

Add all 3 to `DetailComponentPrimitive` union and extraction logic.

## Ribbon buttons

Radial: drop with center at view center, arc point 500mm right.
Diameter: same.
Arc Length: center at view center, radius 500mm, angles 0→90.

## After implementing

1. `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` from `app/`
2. `cd packages/core && pnpm tsc --noEmit`
3. Stage only your changed files (NOT the pre-existing unstaged modifications from git status)
4. Commit: `Add radial, diameter, and arc-length dimension annotations (ANN-06/07/08)`
5. Push

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## What was done

All three dimension annotation types (ANN-06, ANN-07, ANN-08) were added across 7 files in a single commit.

### Files changed

`app/bim_ai/elements.py` — Added `RadialDimensionElem`, `DiameterDimensionElem`, `ArcLengthDimensionElem` after `SpotElevationElem`, and added all three (plus the previously missing `SpotElevationElem`) to the `Element` discriminated union.

`app/bim_ai/commands.py` — Added `CreateRadialDimensionCmd`, `CreateDiameterDimensionCmd`, `CreateArcLengthDimensionCmd` after `CreateSpotElevationCmd`, and wired all three into the `Command` union.

`app/bim_ai/engine.py` — Imported all 6 new names (3 elements + 3 commands) in alphabetical order within their respective import blocks.

`app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Imported all 6 new names, added 3 `case` handlers with validation: radial/diameter check that center and arc_point differ (both coords via `math.isclose`); arc_length additionally checks `radius_mm > 0` and that start/end angles differ.

`packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `'radial_dimension' | 'diameter_dimension' | 'arc_length_dimension'` to the `ElemKind` union, and 3 typed element blocks to the `Element` union.

`packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added 3 primitive types (`RadialDimensionPrimitive`, `DiameterDimensionPrimitive`, `ArcLengthDimensionPrimitive`), added all to `DetailComponentPrimitive`, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.

`packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added `placeRadialDimension`, `placeDiameterDimension`, `placeArcLengthDimension` handler functions and 3 ribbon buttons ("Radial Dim", "Dia. Dim", "Arc Len.") with the specified default geometry.

### Validation
- `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` passes
- `tsc --noEmit` on `packages/core` passes (0 errors)
- Full test suite: 2564 passed, 0 failures

```typescript
  kind: 'radial_dimension'; id: string;

};
export type DiameterDimensionPrimitive = {

export type ArcLengthDimensionPrimitive = {

};
```

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## What was done

All three dimension annotation types (ANN-06, ANN-07, ANN-08) were added across 7 files in a single commit.

### Files changed

`app/bim_ai/elements.py` — Added `RadialDimensionElem`, `DiameterDimensionElem`, `ArcLengthDimensionElem` after `SpotElevationElem`, and added all three (plus the previously missing `SpotElevationElem`) to the `Element` discriminated union.

`app/bim_ai/commands.py` — Added `CreateRadialDimensionCmd`, `CreateDiameterDimensionCmd`, `CreateArcLengthDimensionCmd` after `CreateSpotElevationCmd`, and wired all three into the `Command` union.

`app/bim_ai/engine.py` — Imported all 6 new names (3 elements + 3 commands) in alphabetical order within their respective import blocks.

`app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Imported all 6 new names, added 3 `case` handlers with validation: radial/diameter check that center and arc_point differ (both coords via `math.isclose`); arc_length additionally checks `radius_mm > 0` and that start/end angles differ.

`packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `'radial_dimension' | 'diameter_dimension' | 'arc_length_dimension'` to the `ElemKind` union, and 3 typed element blocks to the `Element` union.

`packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added 3 primitive types (`RadialDimensionPrimitive`, `DiameterDimensionPrimitive`, `ArcLengthDimensionPrimitive`), added all to `DetailComponentPrimitive`, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.

`packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added `placeRadialDimension`, `placeDiameterDimension`, `placeArcLengthDimension` handler functions and 3 ribbon buttons ("Radial Dim", "Dia. Dim", "Arc Len.") with the specified default geometry.

### Validation
- `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` passes
- `tsc --noEmit` on `packages/core` passes (0 errors)
- Full test suite: 2564 passed, 0 failures

input.prompt:
Implement three view-local annotation elements in bim-ai: Spot Coordinate, Spot Slope, and Insulation Annotation. Follow the exact same pattern as `SpotElevationElem` / `CreateSpotElevationCmd` already in the codebase (study those as your template).

---

## 1. Spot Coordinate (`spot_coordinate`)

Shows N/E project coordinates at a picked point.

### elements.py — add after `SpotElevationElem`:
```python
class SpotCoordinateElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-09 — view-local spot coordinate annotation (N/E at a point)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["spot_coordinate"] = "spot_coordinate"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    north_mm: float = Field(alias="northMm")
    east_mm: float = Field(alias="eastMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateSpotCoordinateCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-09 — view-local spot coordinate."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createSpotCoordinate"] = "createSpotCoordinate"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    north_mm: float = Field(alias="northMm")
    east_mm: float = Field(alias="eastMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

---

## 2. Spot Slope (`spot_slope`)

Shows slope percentage or ratio at a picked point (for ramps, sloped floors).

### elements.py:
```python
SpotSlopeFormat = Literal["percent", "ratio", "degree"]

class SpotSlopeElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-10 — view-local spot slope annotation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["spot_slope"] = "spot_slope"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    slope_pct: float = Field(alias="slopePct")
    slope_format: SpotSlopeFormat = Field(default="percent", alias="slopeFormat")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateSpotSlopeCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-10 — view-local spot slope."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createSpotSlope"] = "createSpotSlope"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    slope_pct: float = Field(alias="slopePct")
    slope_format: Literal["percent", "ratio", "degree"] = Field(
        default="percent", alias="slopeFormat"
    )
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

---

## 3. Insulation Annotation (`insulation_annotation`)

A zigzag line annotation showing insulation in wall sections.

### elements.py:
```python
class InsulationAnnotationElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-11 — view-local insulation annotation (zigzag line)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["insulation_annotation"] = "insulation_annotation"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="widthMm", gt=0)
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateInsulationAnnotationCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-11 — view-local insulation annotation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createInsulationAnnotation"] = "createInsulationAnnotation"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="widthMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### Handler validation: start_mm and end_mm must differ (check both x and y via math.isclose).

---

## Files to modify — same 7-file pattern

1. `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — add 3 element classes + `SpotSlopeFormat` literal
2. `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — add 3 command classes + add all to `Command` union
3. `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — import all 6 new names alphabetically
4. `app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — import + 3 case handlers
5. `packages/core/src/index.ts` — 3 kind strings + 3 element type blocks
6. `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — 3 primitive types + extraction
7. `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — 3 buttons

## Frontend primitives

```typescript
export type SpotCoordinatePrimitive = {
  kind: 'spot_coordinate'; id: string;
  positionMm: XY; northMm: number; eastMm: number; colour: string;
};
export type SpotSlopePrimitive = {
  kind: 'spot_slope'; id: string;
  positionMm: XY; slopePct: number;
  slopeFormat: 'percent' | 'ratio' | 'degree'; colour: string;
};
export type InsulationAnnotationPrimitive = {
  kind: 'insulation_annotation'; id: string;
  startMm: XY; endMm: XY; widthMm: number; colour: string;
};
```

## Ribbon buttons

- Spot Coord: drop at view center, northMm: 0, eastMm: 0 — testid `plan-annotate-spot-coordinate`
- Spot Slope: drop at view center, slopePct: 5.0 — testid `plan-annotate-spot-slope`
- Insulation: start 500mm left of center, end 500mm right — testid `plan-annotate-insulation`

## After implementing

1. `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` from `app/`
2. `cd packages/core && pnpm tsc --noEmit`
3. Stage only your changed files (NOT pre-existing unstaged modifications)
4. Commit: `Add spot coordinate, spot slope, and insulation annotations (ANN-09/10/11)`
5. Push

content.text:
All done. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

## What was done

All three view-local annotation elements are implemented following the exact `SpotElevationElem` / `CreateSpotElevationCmd` pattern across 7 files:

**Python backend (app/bim_ai/):**

- `elements.py` — Added `SpotCoordinateElem` (ANN-09), `SpotSlopeFormat` literal, `SpotSlopeElem` (ANN-10), and `InsulationAnnotationElem` (ANN-11) immediately after `SpotElevationElem`.
- `commands.py` — Added `CreateSpotCoordinateCmd`, `CreateSpotSlopeCmd`, `CreateInsulationAnnotationCmd`, and registered all three in the `Command` union alongside `CreateSpotElevationCmd`.
- `engine.py` — Imported all 6 new names (3 Cmds + 3 Elems + `SpotSlopeFormat`) alphabetically in both the commands and elements import blocks.
- `engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Added `import math`, imported all 6 new names, and added 3 `case` handlers. The insulation handler validates that `startMm` and `endMm` differ using `math.isclose` on both x and y. All three handlers validate that `hostViewId` references a `plan_view`, `section_cut`, or `elevation_view`.

**TypeScript frontend:**

- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `'spot_coordinate'`, `'spot_slope'`, `'insulation_annotation'` to the `ElementKind` union and added full element type blocks after the `spot_elevation` block.
- `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added 3 new primitive types (`SpotCoordinatePrimitive`, `SpotSlopePrimitive`, `InsulationAnnotationPrimitive`), added them to the `DetailComponentPrimitive` union, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.
- `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added 3 handler functions (`placeSpotCoordinate`, `placeSpotSlope`, `placeInsulationAnnotation`) and 3 ribbon buttons with the specified test IDs (`plan-annotate-spot-coordinate`, `plan-annotate-spot-slope`, `plan-annotate-insulation`).

**Verification:**
- `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` → `ok`
- `npx tsc --noEmit` in `packages/core` → no errors
- `npx tsc --noEmit` in `packages/web` → only pre-existing environment errors (missing `node`/`vite` type defs), no new errors

content.text:
All done. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

## What was done

All three view-local annotation elements are implemented following the exact `SpotElevationElem` / `CreateSpotElevationCmd` pattern across 7 files:

**Python backend (app/bim_ai/):**

- `elements.py` — Added `SpotCoordinateElem` (ANN-09), `SpotSlopeFormat` literal, `SpotSlopeElem` (ANN-10), and `InsulationAnnotationElem` (ANN-11) immediately after `SpotElevationElem`.
- `commands.py` — Added `CreateSpotCoordinateCmd`, `CreateSpotSlopeCmd`, `CreateInsulationAnnotationCmd`, and registered all three in the `Command` union alongside `CreateSpotElevationCmd`.
- `engine.py` — Imported all 6 new names (3 Cmds + 3 Elems + `SpotSlopeFormat`) alphabetically in both the commands and elements import blocks.
- `engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Added `import math`, imported all 6 new names, and added 3 `case` handlers. The insulation handler validates that `startMm` and `endMm` differ using `math.isclose` on both x and y. All three handlers validate that `hostViewId` references a `plan_view`, `section_cut`, or `elevation_view`.

**TypeScript frontend:**

- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `'spot_coordinate'`, `'spot_slope'`, `'insulation_annotation'` to the `ElementKind` union and added full element type blocks after the `spot_elevation` block.
- `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added 3 new primitive types (`SpotCoordinatePrimitive`, `SpotSlopePrimitive`, `InsulationAnnotationPrimitive`), added them to the `DetailComponentPrimitive` union, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.
- `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added 3 handler functions (`placeSpotCoordinate`, `placeSpotSlope`, `placeInsulationAnnotation`) and 3 ribbon buttons with the specified test IDs (`plan-annotate-spot-coordinate`, `plan-annotate-spot-slope`, `plan-annotate-insulation`).

**Verification:**
- `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` → `ok`
- `npx tsc --noEmit` in `packages/core` → no errors
- `npx tsc --noEmit` in `packages/web` → only pre-existing environment errors (missing `node`/`vite` type defs), no new errors

input.description: Implement MEP pipe and duct elements with pipe and duct legend annotations
input.prompt:
Implement MEP pipe and duct elements plus their legend annotations in bim-ai. The software is used for MEP workflows, so full pipe/duct modeling is needed. Follow existing patterns (study `WallElem`/`CreateWallCmd` as a template for the geometry elements, and `SpotElevationElem`/`CreateSpotElevationCmd` for the legend annotations).

---

## 1. Pipe Element (`pipe`)

A straight pipe segment between two points.

### elements.py — add after `MaskingRegionElem`:
```python
PipeSystemType = Literal[
    "domestic_cold_water", "domestic_hot_water", "sanitary",
    "storm_drainage", "fire_protection", "chilled_water",
    "condenser_water", "heating_hot_water", "other"
]

class PipeElem(BaseModel):
    """MEP-01 — straight pipe segment between two points."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["pipe"] = "pipe"
    id: str
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    elevation_mm: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="elevationMm")
    diameter_mm: float = Field(default=25.0, alias="diameterMm", gt=0)
    system_type: PipeSystemType = Field(default="other", alias="systemType")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    colour: str | None = Field(default=None)
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreatePipeCmd(BaseModel):
    """MEP-01 — create a straight pipe segment."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createPipe"] = "createPipe"
    id: str | None = None
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    elevation_mm: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="elevationMm")
    diameter_mm: float = Field(default=25.0, alias="diameterMm")
    system_type: Literal[
        "domestic_cold_water", "domestic_hot_water", "sanitary",
        "storm_drainage", "fire_protection", "chilled_water",
        "condenser_water", "heating_hot_water", "other"
    ] = Field(default="other", alias="systemType")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    colour: str | None = Field(default=None)
```

Handler (in engine_dispatch_documentation.py or a suitable dispatch file): validate level_id references a LevelElem, validate start_mm != end_mm.

```python
        case CreatePipeCmd():
            pid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if pid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{pid}'")
            if cmd.level_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.level_id], LevelElem):
                raise ValueError("createPipe.levelId must reference a Level")
            import math as _math
            if _math.isclose(cmd.start_mm.x_mm, cmd.end_mm.x_mm) and _math.isclose(cmd.start_mm.y_mm, cmd.end_mm.y_mm):
                raise ValueError("createPipe: startMm and endMm must differ")
            els[pid] = PipeElem(
                kind="pipe",
                id=pid,
                level_id=cmd.level_id,
                start_mm=cmd.start_mm,
                end_mm=cmd.end_mm,
                elevation_mm=cmd.elevation_mm,
                diameter_mm=cmd.diameter_mm,
                system_type=cmd.system_type,
                material_key=cmd.material_key,
                colour=cmd.colour,
            )
```

---

## 2. Duct Element (`duct`)

A straight duct segment between two points.

### elements.py:
```python
DuctSystemType = Literal[
    "supply_air", "return_air", "exhaust_air",
    "outside_air", "other_air", "other"
]
DuctShape = Literal["rectangular", "round", "oval"]

class DuctElem(BaseModel):
    """MEP-02 — straight duct segment between two points."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["duct"] = "duct"
    id: str
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    elevation_mm: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="elevationMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(default=300.0, alias="widthMm", gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="heightMm", gt=0)
    shape: DuctShape = Field(default="rectangular")
    system_type: DuctSystemType = Field(default="other", alias="systemType")
    colour: str | None = Field(default=None)
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateDuctCmd(BaseModel):
    """MEP-02 — create a straight duct segment."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createDuct"] = "createDuct"
    id: str | None = None
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    elevation_mm: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="elevationMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(default=300.0, alias="widthMm")
    height_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="heightMm")
    shape: Literal["rectangular", "round", "oval"] = Field(default="rectangular")
    system_type: Literal[
        "supply_air", "return_air", "exhaust_air",
        "outside_air", "other_air", "other"
    ] = Field(default="other", alias="systemType")
    colour: str | None = Field(default=None)
```

Handler: same validation pattern as pipe (levelId + start!=end).

---

## 3. Pipe Legend (`pipe_legend`)

A view-local legend box listing pipe system types and their colours.

### elements.py:
```python
class PipeLegendEntrySpec(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    system_type: str = Field(alias="systemType")
    label: str
    colour: str

class PipeLegendElem(BaseModel):
    """MEP-03 — view-local pipe legend annotation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["pipe_legend"] = "pipe_legend"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    entries: list[PipeLegendEntrySpec] = Field(default_factory=list)
    title: str = Field(default="Pipe Legend")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreatePipeLegendCmd(BaseModel):
    """MEP-03 — place a pipe legend in a view."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createPipeLegend"] = "createPipeLegend"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    entries: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
    title: str = Field(default="Pipe Legend")
```

Handler: validate host_view_id is plan_view/section_cut/elevation_view. Convert entries dicts to PipeLegendEntrySpec via model_validate.

---

## 4. Duct Legend (`duct_legend`)

Mirror of Pipe Legend for ducts.

### elements.py:
```python
class DuctLegendEntrySpec(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    system_type: str = Field(alias="systemType")
    label: str
    colour: str

class DuctLegendElem(BaseModel):
    """MEP-04 — view-local duct legend annotation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["duct_legend"] = "duct_legend"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    entries: list[DuctLegendEntrySpec] = Field(default_factory=list)
    title: str = Field(default="Duct Legend")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateDuctLegendCmd(BaseModel):
    """MEP-04 — place a duct legend in a view."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createDuctLegend"] = "createDuctLegend"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    entries: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
    title: str = Field(default="Duct Legend")
```

---

## Files to modify

Same 7-file pattern (engine_dispatch_documentation.py for legend annotations; for the pipe/duct geometry elements you can also use engine_dispatch_documentation.py or create a new engine_dispatch_mep.py — whichever you prefer, but if you create a new file, register it in engine_dispatch.py alongside the others):

1. `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — add all 4 elements + 2 entry specs + all literal types
2. `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — add 4 commands + add to `Command` union
3. `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — import all new names
4. Dispatch: add handlers (use engine_dispatch_documentation.py or new engine_dispatch_mep.py registered in engine_dispatch.py)
5. `packages/core/src/index.ts` — 4 kind strings + 4 element type blocks
6. `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — 4 primitive types + extraction for legends (pipe/duct geometry renders via plan canvas, not this module; add only PipeLegendPrimitive + DuctLegendPrimitive)
7. `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — 2 legend buttons (pipe/duct elements are placed via modeling tools, not the annotate ribbon; only the legend annotations go in the ribbon)

## Frontend primitives (for legends only)

```typescript
export type PipeLegendPrimitive = {
  kind: 'pipe_legend'; id: string;
  positionMm: XY; title: string;
  entries: { systemType: string; label: string; colour: string }[];
};
export type DuctLegendPrimitive = {
  kind: 'duct_legend'; id: string;
  positionMm: XY; title: string;
  entries: { systemType: string; label: string; colour: string }[];
};
```

Add `pipe` and `duct` to the core `Element` union with their full shapes. Add the legend primitives to `DetailComponentPrimitive`.

## TypeScript Element types for pipe and duct

```typescript
  | {
      kind: 'pipe';
      id: string;
      levelId: string;
      startMm: XY; endMm: XY;
      elevationMm?: number;
      diameterMm?: number;
      systemType?: string;
      materialKey?: string | null;
      colour?: string | null;
      pinned?: boolean;
    }
  | {
      kind: 'duct';
      id: string;
      levelId: string;
      startMm: XY; endMm: XY;
      elevationMm?: number;
      widthMm?: number;
      heightMm?: number;
      shape?: 'rectangular' | 'round' | 'oval';
      systemType?: string;
      colour?: string | null;
      pinned?: boolean;
    }
```

## Ribbon buttons

- Pipe Legend → `plan-annotate-pipe-legend` — place at view center with 4 default entries (one per common system type, with different colours)
- Duct Legend → `plan-annotate-duct-legend` — place at view center with 3 default entries

Default pipe entries:
```javascript
entries: [
  { systemType: 'domestic_cold_water', label: 'Cold Water', colour: '#0066cc' },
  { systemType: 'domestic_hot_water', label: 'Hot Water', colour: '#cc3300' },
  { systemType: 'sanitary', label: 'Sanitary', colour: '#996633' },
  { systemType: 'fire_protection', label: 'Fire Protection', colour: '#cc0000' },
]
```

Default duct entries:
```javascript
entries: [
  { systemType: 'supply_air', label: 'Supply Air', colour: '#0099cc' },
  { systemType: 'return_air', label: 'Return Air', colour: '#66aacc' },
  { systemType: 'exhaust_air', label: 'Exhaust Air', colour: '#999999' },
]
```

## After implementing

1. `python -c "from bim_ai.engine import Command; print('ok')"` from `app/`
2. `cd packages/core && pnpm tsc --noEmit`
3. Stage only your changed files
4. Commit: `Add MEP pipe and duct elements with pipe/duct legend annotations (MEP-01..04)`
5. Push

content.text:
All done. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

**7 files modified, 430 lines added.**

**Python (backend)**

- `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — Added `PipeSystemType`, `PipeElem` (MEP-01), `DuctSystemType`, `DuctShape`, `DuctElem` (MEP-02), `PipeLegendEntrySpec`, `PipeLegendElem` (MEP-03), `DuctLegendEntrySpec`, `DuctLegendElem` (MEP-04). All four elements added to the `Element` discriminated union.

- `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — Added `CreatePipeCmd`, `CreateDuctCmd`, `CreatePipeLegendCmd`, `CreateDuctLegendCmd`. All four added to the `Command` union.

- `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — Imported all 8 new names (4 commands + 4 elements + 2 entry specs).

- `app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Added handlers for all 4 commands in `try_apply_documentation_command`. Pipe/duct validate `levelId` references a `LevelElem` and that `startMm != endMm`. Legends validate `hostViewId` references `plan_view`, `section_cut`, or `elevation_view`, and convert entry dicts via `model_validate`.

**TypeScript (frontend)**

- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `pipe_legend` and `duct_legend` to `ElemKind`; added inline `pipe`, `duct`, `pipe_legend`, and `duct_legend` shapes to the `Element` union.

- `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added `PipeLegendPrimitive` and `DuctLegendPrimitive` types, added both to `DetailComponentPrimitive` union, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.

- `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added `placePipeLegend` and `placeDuctLegend` handlers (with 4 and 3 default entries respectively) and corresponding ribbon buttons (`data-testid="plan-annotate-pipe-legend"` and `data-testid="plan-annotate-duct-legend"`).

```typescript
  kind: 'duct_legend'; id: string;

};
```

content.text:
All done. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

**7 files modified, 430 lines added.**

**Python (backend)**

- `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — Added `PipeSystemType`, `PipeElem` (MEP-01), `DuctSystemType`, `DuctShape`, `DuctElem` (MEP-02), `PipeLegendEntrySpec`, `PipeLegendElem` (MEP-03), `DuctLegendEntrySpec`, `DuctLegendElem` (MEP-04). All four elements added to the `Element` discriminated union.

- `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — Added `CreatePipeCmd`, `CreateDuctCmd`, `CreatePipeLegendCmd`, `CreateDuctLegendCmd`. All four added to the `Command` union.

- `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — Imported all 8 new names (4 commands + 4 elements + 2 entry specs).

- `app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_documentation.py` — Added handlers for all 4 commands in `try_apply_documentation_command`. Pipe/duct validate `levelId` references a `LevelElem` and that `startMm != endMm`. Legends validate `hostViewId` references `plan_view`, `section_cut`, or `elevation_view`, and convert entry dicts via `model_validate`.

**TypeScript (frontend)**

- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Added `pipe_legend` and `duct_legend` to `ElemKind`; added inline `pipe`, `duct`, `pipe_legend`, and `duct_legend` shapes to the `Element` union.

- `packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts` — Added `PipeLegendPrimitive` and `DuctLegendPrimitive` types, added both to `DetailComponentPrimitive` union, and added extraction branches in `extractDetailComponentPrimitives`.

- `packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx` — Added `placePipeLegend` and `placeDuctLegend` handlers (with 4 and 3 default entries respectively) and corresponding ribbon buttons (`data-testid="plan-annotate-pipe-legend"` and `data-testid="plan-annotate-duct-legend"`).

input.prompt:
Implement four annotation/grouping features in bim-ai. Follow the `SpotElevationElem` / `CreateSpotElevationCmd` pattern as a template (already in the codebase).

---

## 1. Detail Component (`detail_component`)

A predefined 2D component placed in a view. Simplified: choose from a catalog of built-in shape types rather than loading external families.

### elements.py:
```python
DetailComponentShape = Literal[
    "us_wide_flange_beam", "concrete_column_square", "concrete_column_round",
    "steel_angle", "steel_channel", "bolt", "weld_symbol",
    "break_line", "centerline_end"
]

class DetailComponentElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-17 — view-local 2D detail component (predefined shape)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["detail_component"] = "detail_component"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    component_shape: DetailComponentShape = Field(alias="componentShape")
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    scale: float = Field(default=1.0, gt=0)
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateDetailComponentCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-17 — place a 2D detail component."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createDetailComponent"] = "createDetailComponent"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    component_shape: Literal[
        "us_wide_flange_beam", "concrete_column_square", "concrete_column_round",
        "steel_angle", "steel_channel", "bolt", "weld_symbol",
        "break_line", "centerline_end"
    ] = Field(alias="componentShape")
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    scale: float = Field(default=1.0)
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

Handler: validate host_view_id, no extra validation needed.

---

## 2. Repeating Detail (`repeating_detail`)

A pattern of detail components repeated along a line at a fixed spacing.

### elements.py:
```python
class RepeatingDetailElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-18 — view-local repeating detail component pattern along a line."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["repeating_detail"] = "repeating_detail"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    component_shape: DetailComponentShape = Field(alias="componentShape")
    spacing_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="spacingMm", gt=0)
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateRepeatingDetailCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-18 — create a repeating detail component pattern."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createRepeatingDetail"] = "createRepeatingDetail"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    component_shape: Literal[
        "us_wide_flange_beam", "concrete_column_square", "concrete_column_round",
        "steel_angle", "steel_channel", "bolt", "weld_symbol",
        "break_line", "centerline_end"
    ] = Field(alias="componentShape")
    spacing_mm: float = Field(default=200.0, alias="spacingMm")
    colour: str = Field(default="#202020")
```

Handler: validate host_view_id, validate start_mm != end_mm (via math.isclose).

---

## 3. Detail Group (`detail_group`)

A named group of detail element IDs within a view.

### elements.py:
```python
class DetailGroupElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-19 — named group of view-local detail elements."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["detail_group"] = "detail_group"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    name: str = Field(default="Group")
    member_ids: list[str] = Field(alias="memberIds")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateDetailGroupCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-19 — create a named group of detail elements."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createDetailGroup"] = "createDetailGroup"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    name: str = Field(default="Group")
    member_ids: list[str] = Field(alias="memberIds")
```

Handler: validate host_view_id, validate member_ids is non-empty.

---

## 4. Color Fill Legend (`color_fill_legend`)

A legend viewport showing the room color scheme in a view (the color fill system already exists via `RoomColorSchemeElem`; this places a visible legend box in the view).

### elements.py:
```python
class ColorFillLegendElem(BaseModel):
    """ANN-20 — view-local color fill legend box (room colour scheme legend)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["color_fill_legend"] = "color_fill_legend"
    id: str
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    scheme_parameter: str = Field(default="Name", alias="schemeParameter")
    title: str = Field(default="Color Fill Legend")
```

### commands.py:
```python
class CreateColorFillLegendCmd(BaseModel):
    """ANN-20 — place a color fill legend in a view."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createColorFillLegend"] = "createColorFillLegend"
    id: str | None = None
    host_view_id: str = Field(alias="hostViewId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    scheme_parameter: str = Field(default="Name", alias="schemeParameter")
    title: str = Field(default="Color Fill Legend")
```

## Files to modify — same 7-file pattern

1. `app/bim_ai/elements.py` — 4 element classes + `DetailComponentShape` literal
2. `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — 4 command classes + add all to `Command` union

## Frontend primitives

```typescript
export type DetailComponentPrimitiveSingle = {
  kind: 'detail_component'; id: string;

  rotationDeg: number; scale: number; colour: string;
};
export type RepeatingDetailPrimitive = {
```

Note: avoid naming conflicts — `DetailComponentPrimitive` already exists as a union type; name this `DetailComponentSinglePrimitive` or `PlacedDetailComponentPrimitive` to avoid collision. Be careful with this naming.
